name: New Potatoes with Green/Gold Topping
recipeIngredient: 3 handfuls of fresh spinach leaves, stems removed
2 teaspoons Dijon mustard (or 1 part mayo mixed with 2 parts Dijon mustard)
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil potatoes until tender.
2. Rinse saucepan and return to moderate heat; add 2 teaspoons mayonnaise or slaw dressing to melt in pan.
3. Meanwhile, rinse spinach and tear each leaf into three or four pieces.
4. Add the spinach leaves with a bit of water clinging to them to the mayo in the saucepan.
5. Cook uncovered, along with the green onions, until barely wilted. Stir often.
6. Break the eggs over the spinach-onion mixture and stir slightly to scramble as the eggs set.
7. To serve, use a fork to break open each hot potato and mash to form a solid "bed" on each plate. Salt the potato layer lightly and dot with Dijon mustard; spread evenly.
8. Sprinkle with a little shredded Monterey Jack cheese. Top with scrambled spinach-egg-onion mixture in an even layer. Salt lightly, if desired.
9. Sprinkle remaining shredded cheese on top.
NUTRITION ANALYSIS: 1 serving has 638 calories, 31.4 g fat, 508 mg cholesterol. To reduce fat and cholesterol, use egg substitute instead of whole eggs; buy a skim-milk mozzarella instead of Monterey Jack cheese, and use less in the topping. If sodium is a concern, this is still tasty without any table salt.
